variantExpectations: reads as a close visual port of MapTiler Topo while remaining a Tasmania-local preview style backed only by committed local sources
preserves the stronger topo contour and road hierarchy feel without reintroducing remote tiles, sprites, or glyph dependencies

expectations: terrain relief shading is visible across the ridge and valley terrain west of Hobart
settlement labels such as Grove, Collinsvale, or Glenorchy are legible against the pale topo land palette
the low-zoom map reads as a topo-forward reference style rather than the greener Outdoor variant
no basemap peak or summit labels appear
contours are visible without overwhelming the basemap
place labels include Lake St Clair or Derwent Bridge
named water labels include Lake St Clair or Lake King William
road labels include Lyell Highway or Lake St Clair Road
the tile still reads as a close MapTiler Topo port even though it is limited to Tasmania-local sources
no basemap peak or summit labels appear
terrain relief shading remains visible under the local track network
track and path detail are present but remain secondary to the topo contour and road structure
track or path labels include examples such as Organ Pipes Track or Myrtle Gully Track
waterway labels such as New Town Rivulet or Guy Fawkes Rivulet are legible where space allows
no basemap peak or summit labels appear despite mountain_peak source data in this tile

variantExpectations: reads as a close visual port of MapTiler Outdoor while remaining a Tasmania-local preview style backed only by committed local sources
preserves the greener Outdoor palette and trail-forward emphasis without depending on upstream MapTiler-hosted assets

expectations: terrain relief shading is visible across the ridge and valley terrain west of Hobart
the greener Outdoor land palette is obvious compared with the MapTiler Topo variant
settlement labels such as Grove, Collinsvale, or Glenorchy remain legible over the greener background
no basemap peak or summit labels appear
contours stay visible while the greener Outdoor land styling remains dominant
place labels include Lake St Clair or Derwent Bridge
named water labels include Lake St Clair or Lake King William
the tile reads as a close MapTiler Outdoor port within the limits of Tasmania-local source coverage
no basemap peak or summit labels appear
terrain relief shading remains visible under the local track network
track and path detail are more visually prominent than in the MapTiler Topo variant
track or path labels include examples such as Organ Pipes Track or Myrtle Gully Track
waterway labels such as New Town Rivulet or Guy Fawkes Rivulet are legible where space allows
no basemap peak or summit labels appear despite mountain_peak source data in this tile
